passions_detected.false_judgement: That children's non-cooperation is a genuine evil requiring anger to correct
That being the 'good guy' responsible for cousins is necessary for identity
That not being there for cousin constitutes personal failure
That anger reveals cowardice unless willing to escalate to violence

causal_observations.description: Shouting at kids: the impression of disrespect triggers immediate impulse to anger before reasoning can intervene. This is the primary horme failure.
Cousin situation: assented to the false judgement that not reaching out = personal failure, rather than recognising the complexity of the situation.
Key growth: 'vitriolic intent is just as strong' even when shouting is reduced — delivery changed but underlying passion persists.

value_observations.observed_as: deeply held identity marker driving behaviour
understood intellectually — lower heart rate, element of surprise, professional perception

virtue_observations.evidence: Recognises anger as false strength. Understands peacefulness as the genuinely courageous position.
Morning journalling as daily practice. Still needs testing and reflection to reverse ingrained impulses.
Root cause analysis of cousin situation — identifies multiple false judgements. Can separate passion from source.

key_passages.summary: Shouting at kids — 'they will always remember how I made them feel' — then the breakthrough of coaxing with encouragement getting the job done
Cousin crisis and betrayal source — passion of needing to be the good guy, source in being robbed by close friend
Key insight on delivery vs intent — 'shouting may be less but the vitriolic intent is just as strong' — recognises the praxis change hasn't reached synkatathesis
